title: Shared field definitions
description: Domain-neutral, reusable $defs shared by manifest, invoke, completion event, operation status, and callback-response schemas. This document defines no top-level schema of its own; it is only ever consumed via $ref into individual $defs entries so that container schemas (which declare additionalProperties: false) remain self-contained and unambiguous.

contractVersion.description: Wire protocol version. 1 = synchronous-only contract. 2 = adds the deferred completion protocol (callback events, operation status polling, browser continuation).

eventId.description: Opaque, plugin-assigned identifier for a single completion event. Used for callback idempotency.

operationId.description: Opaque, plugin-assigned identifier for a deferred operation.

invocationId.description: Opaque, host-assigned identifier for a single invoke call.

pluginId.description: Opaque identifier for a plugin, unique within the host's plugin registry.

actionId.description: Opaque identifier for a single action declared in a plugin manifest, unique within that plugin.

semver.description: Semantic version string for a plugin's own release, independent of contract_version.

sequenceNonNegative.description: Monotonically increasing counter scoped to one operation_id. Non-negative so that a synchronous initial awaiting_user operation status snapshot may report 0 before any callback has been delivered.

pluginState.description: Plugin-reportable state. Host-only states (pending, ok, expired) are never valid here; they exist solely in the host's internal bookkeeping and MUST NOT appear in any plugin-authored event or status body.

pluginStateAccepted.description: Subset of pluginState a plugin may report synchronously in the invoke-accepted response for a deferred action; terminal states cannot be known that early.

pluginStateTerminal.description: Subset of pluginState representing terminal outcomes. Used by the v1 synchronous invoke response, which always completes immediately and therefore can never report awaiting_user or running.

sequencePositive.description: Monotonically increasing counter scoped to one operation_id, strictly positive. Used by completion events delivered over the callback: sequence 0 is reserved for the synchronous, non-callback initial operation status snapshot (see sequenceNonNegative) and is never sent as a callback event.

resultMode.description: How the result artifact (if any) is delivered. 'none' = no external artifact (result_url MUST be absent). 'url' = result_url MUST be present and is the canonical result location.

occurredAt.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of when the reported state transition occurred, as observed by the plugin.

progress.description: Optional coarse progress indicator. additionalProperties is false: this object carries no domain-specific sub-fields.

externalId.description: Opaque identifier assigned by an external system the plugin delegates work to (e.g. a job queue). Printable, non-whitespace ASCII only; domain-neutral and provider-agnostic.

provider.description: Opaque, conservative-ASCII label identifying the downstream provider/subsystem that produced the event, if any.

errorMessage.description: Human-readable error detail. Not machine-parsed; no character-set restriction beyond length.

httpsUrl.description: Absolute HTTPS URL, e.g. result_url or launch_url.

epochSeconds.description: NumericDate as defined by RFC 7519 (JWT): a JSON number representing seconds since 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z UTC, ignoring leap seconds.

jwtId.description: Opaque, single-use identifier for one browser continuation assertion (JWT 'jti' claim). MUST be consumed (rejected on reuse) exactly once by the verifying party.

treeId.description: Opaque, host-assigned identifier for the content tree a browser continuation assertion is scoped to. Domain-neutral: this repository imposes no meaning on the identifier beyond opacity and uniqueness within the host.

assertionPurpose.description: Fixed audience-confusion guard for the browser continuation interaction-assertion JWT. This claim's value never varies; its presence and constant value let a verifier reject tokens minted for any other purpose even if they otherwise share the signing key, issuer, and audience.

csrfToken.description: Opaque, single-use-session-scoped anti-CSRF token issued alongside the browser continuation session cookie. Callers MUST echo it back in the X-ContentTree-CSRF-Token header on subsequent state-changing requests within that browser session.

jwtCompact.description: A JWS Compact Serialization string (RFC 7515): three base64url segments separated by '.'. Used for the browser continuation interaction-assertion, always RS256-signed.

hostApiBaseUrl.description: Absolute origin (scheme + host[:port], no path/query/fragment) at which the host exposes its plugin-host API for this invocation, so the plugin never has to guess or construct the callback destination itself (anti-SSRF). HTTPS is required except for the explicit http://localhost / http://127.0.0.1 allowance, which exists solely to support local conformance testing and MUST NOT be honored by production hosts/plugins.
